dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4494 IS: American Cures Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Durbin (for himself, Mr. Blumenthal, Ms. Duckworth, and Mr. Van Hollen) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Appropriations

form.official-title: To prioritize funding for an expanded and sustained national investment in biomedical research.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the American Cures Act.

subsection.text: There are hereby authorized to be appropriated, and appropriated, out of any monies in the Treasury not otherwise appropriated, the following:

paragraph.text: For the National Institutes of Health at the Department of Health and Human Services—

subparagraph.text: for fiscal year 2037 and each fiscal year thereafter, the amount appropriated under this paragraph for the previous fiscal year, increased by the percentage increase (if any), during the previous fiscal year, in the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

paragraph.header: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
paragraph.text: For the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention at the Department of Health and Human Services—

subparagraph.text: for fiscal year 2037 and each fiscal year thereafter, the amount appropriated under this paragraph for the previous fiscal year, increased by the percentage increase (if any), during the previous fiscal year, in the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

paragraph.header: Research, development, test, and evaluation program of the Department of Defense health program
paragraph.text: For the research, development, test, and evaluation program of the Department of Defense health program—

subparagraph.text: for fiscal year 2037 and each fiscal year thereafter, the amount appropriated under this paragraph for the previous fiscal year, increased by the percentage increase (if any), during the previous fiscal year, in the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

paragraph.header: Medical and prosthetics research program of the Department of Veterans Affairs
paragraph.text: For the medical and prosthetics research program of the Department of Veterans Affairs—

subparagraph.text: for fiscal year 2037 and each fiscal year thereafter, the amount appropriated under this paragraph for the previous fiscal year, increased by the percentage increase (if any), during the previous fiscal year, in the Consumer Price Index for all urban consumers published by the Bureau of Labor Statistics.

subsection.text: Amounts appropriated under subsection (a) shall remain available until expended.

paragraph.header: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
paragraph.text: The term Centers for Disease Control and Prevention means the appropriations accounts that support the various institutes, offices, and centers that make up the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

paragraph.header: Research, development, test, and evaluation program of the Department of Defense health program
paragraph.text: The term research, development, test, and evaluation program of the Department of Defense health program means the appropriations accounts that support the various institutes, offices, and centers that make up the research, development, test, and evaluation program of the Department of Defense health program.

paragraph.header: Medical and prosthetics research program of the Department of Veterans Affairs
paragraph.text: The term medical and prosthetics research program of the Department of Veterans Affairs means the appropriations accounts that support the various institutes, offices, and centers that make up the medical and prosthetics research program of the Department of Veterans Affairs.
The term National Institutes of Health means the appropriations accounts that support the various institutes, offices, and centers that make up the National Institutes of Health.

subsection.header: Exemption of certain appropriations from sequestration

paragraph.text: Section 255(g)(1)(A) of the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act (2 U.S.C. 905(g)(1)(A)) is amended by inserting after Advances to the Unemployment Trust Fund and Other Funds (16–0327–0–1–600). the following:

clause.text: Appropriations under the American Cures Act.

paragraph.text: The amendment made by this section shall apply to any sequestration order issued under the Balanced Budget and Emergency Deficit Control Act of 1985 (2 U.S.C. 900 et seq.) on or after the date of enactment of this Act.
The budgetary effects of this section shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go Act of 2010 (2 U.S.C. 933(d)).
The budgetary effects of this section shall not be entered on any PAYGO scorecard maintained for purposes of section 4106 of H. Con. Res. 71 (115th Congress).
